strategic_profile.executive_summary: George Mason University is Virginia's largest public research university, holding a Tier 1 (R1) designation. It leverages its strategic location near Washington, D.C., to provide unparalleled internship and career pipelines in tech, government, and policy.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic review with a focus on accessibility, though heavily numbers-driven for specific selective programs like Computer Science and Nursing.

analyst_takeaways.insight: While the overall acceptance rate is 87.4%, programs in the College of Engineering and Computing and the School of Nursing require stringent math and science prerequisites for direct admission.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants to competitive majors must ensure their high school transcripts reflect strong STEM performance or utilize the 'Undeclared' pathway and transfer internally.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university is need-blind for admissions but meets only an estimated 54-61% of demonstrated financial need.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students requiring significant financial aid should apply by the November 1 Early Action deadline to maximize consideration for automatic merit scholarships.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Strong integration with regional employers ensures high employment rates, particularly for Information Technology, Computer Science, and Public Policy majors.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking careers in federal contracting, defense, or policy will find GMU provides a near-unrivaled geographic and networking advantage.

analyst_takeaways.insight: GMU is an AANAPISI-designated institution with 57% BIPOC students and roughly 26% first-generation students, maintaining a strong, pragmatic, and diverse community.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants from diverse or non-traditional backgrounds will find a highly supportive infrastructure, particularly through the First-Gen+ Center.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Historically a commuter school, GMU has rapidly expanded its residential facilities. The social scene is pragmatic and heavily focused on professional development, over 500 student organizations, and D.C. excursions, rather than traditional college-town partying.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located just 15 miles from Washington, D.C., the campus offers direct shuttle access to the Metro system, seamlessly connecting students to Capitol Hill, federal agencies, and the Virginia Technology Corridor.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution generating over $262 million in annual research expenditures, GMU offers robust undergraduate research opportunities, notably at the new Fuse building at Mason Square in Arlington, focusing on AI and cybersecurity.

current_status.test_policy_details: Score-optional for most majors. Students not submitting test scores must submit an additional supplemental essay explaining their motivation for higher education.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist acceptance rate dropped to 17.5% from 22.8% the previous year, highlighting increased yield protection and competitiveness for borderline candidates.

gpa.notes: GPA data specific to 25th/75th percentiles is not publicly separated; average admitted GPA is 3.69.

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Computing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students with strong quantitative backgrounds seeking access to the D.C. tech corridor and defense contractors. High math proficiency is strictly required.
colleges.housing_profile: Residential learning communities available for engineering students.
colleges.student_archetype: Career-focused tech enthusiasts looking to leverage federal and corporate pipelines.

majors.name: Computer Science
prerequisite_courses: Four years of high school math
One course more advanced than Algebra II
majors.special_requirements: Direct admission requires a minimum 550 Math SAT or 24 Math ACT, OR grades of B or better in all high school math courses up to and including Algebra II.

core_courses: CS 405 Ethics and Law in Computing

majors.notes: Students who do not meet direct admit criteria are placed into the CEC Undeclared major and must complete CS 112/211 and MATH 113/114/125 with a B or better to declare.

majors.name: Information Technology
majors.special_requirements: No selective admission requirements for freshman applicants.
majors.notes: Highly popular major providing pathways into cybersecurity and network architecture without the heavy theoretical math of the CS degree.

colleges.name: College of Public Health
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for future healthcare professionals; the BSN is highly competitive and requires exceptional academic standing.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Requires a second review of freshman applications by the School of Nursing admission committee.
majors.notes: Qualified applicants admitted to the university but not the School of Nursing will be placed as undeclared majors.

colleges.name: Costello College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Perfect for students targeting consulting, finance, and management roles in the D.C. area.

majors.name: Business Administration and Management
majors.notes: The most popular undergraduate major at George Mason University.

colleges.name: College of Humanities and Social Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students targeting pre-law, counseling, or public service.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: One of the largest programs by graduation volume.

majors.name: Criminal Justice
majors.notes: Highly specialized program leveraging connections with local D.C. and federal law enforcement.

application_deadlines.notes: Applying by this deadline ensures priority consideration for the Honors College and Merit-Based Scholarships (including the University Scholars Award).
Honors College consideration is on a space-available basis only.

supplemental_requirements.details: Required 400-word essay: 'What is your motivation for pursuing higher education? Why do you believe George Mason University is the right institution for you?' (Optional for students submitting test scores, but highly encouraged).

One 500-word essay choosing from two prompts: (1) Address an unanswered question or unresolved challenge you want to tackle at Mason, or (2) Discuss a time you were moved to act by art, literature, or a speech.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: As an institution highly focused on first-generation and upward mobility success, authentic narratives detailing personal challenges and career pragmatism resonate well.

major_selection_tactics: Students with lower math grades should avoid applying directly to Computer Science or Engineering, as direct admission is strictly gated by math grades (B or better) and rigor.
Consider applying to Information Technology instead of Computer Science if you want a tech-focused career but lack the stringent Pre-Calculus/Calculus background required for CEC direct admission.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: George Mason heavily utilizes an alternate major strategy. Applicants to selective majors (like CS, Engineering, or Nursing) who do not meet direct admit criteria are automatically considered for admission into an 'Undeclared' major (e.g., CEC Undeclared). From there, students can internally transfer into their desired major after completing foundational courses (e.g., CS 112, MATH 113) with a 2.75 GPA.

financials.tuition_model: Per-credit or annual flat rate for full-time enrollment (12-15 credits).

scholarships.name: University Scholars Award
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition over four years. Highest academic distinction offered.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic consideration when applying to the Honors College by the EA deadline.

scholarships.name: Mason Merit Scholarships
scholarships.application_method: Automatic consideration with Early Action application.

scholarships.name: George Mason Undergraduate International Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Up to $20,000 per year, renewable annually.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic for directly admitted F-1 visa international students.

scholarships.name: Mason Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.application_method: Separate application via the Mason Scholarship Application portal (requires FAFSA/VASA).

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for many AP exams, generally requiring a score of 3, 4, or 5.
exceptions: CS 112 requires a 4 on AP Computer Science A.
CS 211 requires a 5 on AP Computer Science A.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to satisfy major prerequisites, Mason Core requirements, and elective credits.

ib_policy.general_rule: Accepts IB Higher Level (HL) and some Standard Level (SL) exams.

tools: Guaranteed Admission Agreement (GAA) with Northern Virginia Community College (NOVA)
transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students with 45 or more hours of transfer coursework are generally not permitted to take additional coursework at another institution. Max transfer credits apply.

what_it_takes: Meeting the math rigor requirements for CEC applicants.
Applying Early Action to secure merit aid and Honors College consideration.
A strong supplemental essay emphasizing pragmatism, career goals, and a specific desire to leverage the D.C. area.
essay_tips: Connect your personal motivations directly to specific GMU resources (e.g., the proximity to D.C., specific research centers like the Fuse building).
If answering the Honors prompt, focus on an unresolved challenge that aligns with your intended major and future career path.
red_flags: Applying RD (February 1) and expecting merit aid or Honors College admission.
Applying for direct-admit CS/Engineering without having completed grades of B or better in advanced math.
insights: The campus has historically been heavily commuter-based, meaning weekend social life can be quiet; however, the residential population is growing rapidly.
Transferring from NOVA (Northern Virginia Community College) is extremely common and fully integrated into the campus culture.
